B.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
B.inline_verdict: Plausibly compelling mix-shift trade; progress to ESA with DC moat + liquidity gates

blocks.text: Progress to ESA. Hexatronic is a mix-shift trade: DC segment (+37% organic 2025, 15%+ EBITA margin, asset-light) is displacing legacy FTTH (6.4% margin, overcapacity). The thesis is Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT — hyperscaler infrastructure buildout + services pivot creates a credible path to SEK 11bn revenue / 12% EBITA margin by 2028. Two material gates before sizing: (i) DC competitive moat durability — is this the next boom-bust or a structurally defensible niche? (ii) M&A execution — the 2028 target requires 3-6 acquisitions/year at 5-7x EBITDA.

The investment case rests on three legs: (1) DC segment scales to SEK 3bn by 2028 (from ~SEK 1.4bn in 2025) via organic growth + bolt-on M&A, (2) Harsh Environment provides defensive ballast with 11% organic growth driven by NATO defence budgets and offshore energy, (3) Fibre Solutions stabilises via SEK 120m cost programme and eventual BEAD-driven US recovery. The fulcrum question is whether HTRO's mid-market DC niche (Tier-2 cloud, regional DCs, edge computing) is defensible against Corning/Prysmian if hyperscaler capex normalises.

items.text: PRIMARY driver: DC segment scaling — fulcrum. Q4-25 organic growth +62% confirms momentum; 15.3% EBITA margin is 2x group average.

sub.text: DC + HE = ~40% of sales but ~60% of adj. EBITA by Q4-25. Mix shift is the numbers story.
Cloud/hyperscaler = 40% of DC sales; enterprise = 60%. Dual customer base reduces single-customer risk.
Management targets 3-6 DC acquisitions/year at 5-7x EBITDA, EV <EUR 50m. Pipeline appears real given US market fragmentation.

items.text: SECONDARY driver: BEAD programme materialisation — triggers legacy FTTH volume recovery in H2-26.

sub.text: $42.5bn US subsidy; 50 of 56 state proposals approved. 74% of early allocations still going to fibre despite technology-neutral rule change.
BEAD impact timing has been pushed right repeatedly. Management now guides H2-26; sell-side expects 2027 for meaningful revenue contribution.

items.text: TERTIARY driver: Harsh Environment defence/offshore tailwind — structural, secular, non-cyclical ballast.

sub.text: HE segment: +11% organic 2025, 11.2% EBITA margin rising toward 15% target. JOWO acquisition (EUR 11.8m, 4.7x EBITDA) adds European defence exposure.
NATO spending commitments provide 5-10 year visibility. Rochester Cable operational improvements are margin-accretive.

blocks.text: Key risk: the DC boom-bust parallel. Sell-side (Redeye) explicitly warns that hyperscaler capex could exhibit the same boom-bust as FTTH. The FTTH cycle saw Hexatronic's revenue surge 33% CAGR then collapse — if DC follows the same pattern, the 2028 targets are unachievable. Mitigant: DC business is services-led (design, installation, day-two) vs. FTTH which was product-distribution; services have higher switching costs and recurring revenue characteristics. This distinction needs pressure-testing at ESA.

D.title: COMPONENTS
D.inline_verdict: 10 INVALIDATION ACHs armed with HTRO-specific triggers; 3 KOQs for ESA

subsections.title: KDICs — Key Drivers of Investment Case

items.text: **FULCRUM #1: DC segment competitive moat durability** — Can HTRO's mid-market DC services niche sustain 20%+ organic growth and 15%+ margins against Corning/Prysmian pivot? KD Rating: YELLOW.

**FULCRUM #2: M&A execution quality** — Requires 3-6 acquisitions/year at 5-7x EBITDA with successful integration. Weterings closure (acquired 2021 for EUR 5.6m, closed 2025) is a capital destruction flag.

sub.text: KD Rating: ORANGE.

items.text: **KEY DRIVER #3: FTTH recovery trajectory** — BEAD impact timing (H2-26 per mgmt, 2027 per SS). Fibre Solutions margin rebound from 6.4% to 10% target. KD Rating: YELLOW.
**KEY DRIVER #4: CEO quality (Fröberg)** — 12 months in; conservative guidance reset + Q3-Q4 beats. M&A background (Ahlsell) is relevant. Untested in fibre industry downturn. KD Rating: YELLOW.
**KEY DRIVER #5: EPS bridge to 2028** — SEK 515m EBITA (7.2% margin, 2025) → SEK 1.32bn EBITA (12%, 2028). 2.6x growth required.

sub.text: Achievable if DC sustains + M&A executes + costs land. KD Rating: YELLOW.

items.text: **KHF-1: Balance sheet health.** Net debt SEK 1,582m, leverage 1.9x EBITDA. Covenant headroom adequate. No immediate refinancing risk.
**KHF-2: Cash conversion.** Q4-25 operating cash flow conversion 235%. Strong WC management (inventory -SEK 144m, AR -SEK 94m). Payback on restructuring ~1.1 years.
**KHF-3: Governance.** No controlling shareholder (largest: Handelsbanken Fonder 9.3%). Board + mgmt own ~13%. Erik Selin related-party lease (SEK 7m/year) is minor. No red flags.
**KA-1: Assumption — hyperscaler capex sustains.** Modal case assumes 15% CAGR in cloud DC market over N5Y. If AI monetisation disappoints, capex could pause (bear case trigger).
**KA-2: Assumption — HTRO can acquire at 5-7x EBITDA.** If PE buyers inflate DC service company multiples, M&A engine stalls.
**KA-3: Assumption — Fibre Solutions stabilises at 10% EBITA.** Requires cost programme delivery + BEAD-driven volume recovery. If overcapacity persists, margin stays at 6-7%.
**KC-1: DC switching costs.** No disclosed customer lock-in periods. Microduct is not proprietary — CommMesh, Corning, others offer similar systems. 'Flexibility' helps the customer, not HTRO.

sub.text: Is the moat real or perceived?

items.text: **KC-2: M&A integration model.** Decentralised integration (preserving entrepreneurial culture) works at small scale. Does it scale to 12+ acquisitions over 3 years without creating coordination failure?
**KC-3: FTTH structural floor.** Is the FTTH business permanently impaired (negative terminal growth per Redeye bear case) or cyclically depressed with secular catch-up in US/UK/Germany?

**KR-1: DC boom-bust risk.** Redeye explicitly warns DC hyperscaler demand shares FTTH boom-bust characteristics. If capex pauses, HTRO's DC segment could decelerate from +37% to <10% within 2-3 quarters. Probability:

sub.text: 20-30% over N2Y.

items.text: **KR-2: Customer concentration.** Top 10 = ~50% of sales. Loss or procurement shift by 1-2 major hyperscalers could cause a revenue miss of 5-10%.
**KR-3: Corning/Prysmian competitive pivot.** If Tier-1 carrier demand stalls, these giants redirect excess capacity and salesforce to Tier-2/3 alt-nets — HTRO's core customer base.

sub.text: Scale advantage is overwhelming in a price war.

items.text: **KR-4: CEO execution risk.** Fröberg lacks fibre industry experience. 12 months is insufficient to judge turnaround capability in a structurally challenged segment.
**KR-5: FX headwinds.** Strong SEK vs USD/EUR erodes translated revenue. 9% negative FX impact on sales in late 2025.
**KR-6: Glass preform supply vulnerability.** Not vertically integrated. Relies on third-party glass producers. If synchronised demand boom hits, supply shortages could cap revenue growth.

**KR-7: LEO satellite substitution.** Starlink/Amazon Kuiper receiving BEAD funding eligibility. Gen-3 satellites at 1 Tbit/s could narrow fibre performance gap in rural/remote markets, capping FTTH TAM.
**KPO-1: BEAD acceleration.** If US fibre deployment ramps faster than expected, Fibre Solutions could recover to mid-teens margins, adding SEK 200-300m incremental EBITA.
**KPO-2: Edge DC proliferation.** Next wave of infrastructure (distributed edge DCs closer to end users) could extend DC growth runway beyond 2028.

sub.text: HTRO's local service model is well-positioned for fragmented edge deployment.

items.text: **KPO-3: Multiple re-rating on mix shift.** As DC/HE reaches 50% of revenue, the blended multiple should re-rate from FTTH-legacy 10-12x toward services 15-18x.

sub.text: This is the biggest source of TSR asymmetry.

items.text: **KPO-4: Defence budget escalation.** Geopolitical tensions (Iran, Russia) could accelerate NATO defence spending beyond 2% commitments, providing additional HE segment tailwind.

blocks.text: _{10_INVALIDATION_ACHS} **Live-case invalidation discipline.** These ten one-strike rules apply __while we own this case__ and are explicitly different from the screening thresholds used for new investments. Any single trip → __APM escalates to Richard__ with a sell/trim recommendation; no waiting for a second confirmation. State of each threshold is tracked below._
**Single-event invalidations (rules 1–5)** — fundamental triggers that fire on a single condition.

items.text: **1. Top-line invalidation** — One __probable or actual near-term revenue cut__ to SS/G caused by an exogenous problem. HTRO-specific: DC segment organic growth drops below +10% for two consecutive quarters,

sub.text: OR Fibre Solutions revenue declines >15% YoY without BEAD offset.

items.text: **2. Cockroach invalidation** — __One actual AND one probable__, OR __three probable/likely__ current/near-term problems. HTRO-specific: (i) M&A write-down (second Weterings-type capital destruction), (ii) DC customer loss (top-3 hyperscaler terminates or

sub.text: significantly reduces procurement), (iii) FTTH overcapacity worsens despite cost programme.

items.text: **3. Ditherer invalidation** — __Deterioration in operator assessment__ AND one or more problems. HTRO-specific: CEO Fröberg loses credibility (guidance miss >20%, strategic reversal on DC pivot, unexplained departure of key

sub.text: DC segment leaders) AND concurrent FTTH deterioration or DC deceleration.

items.text: **4. Cyclical invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY a plausible+ near-term revenue cut caused by threshold+ exogenous problem. HTRO-specific:

sub.text: SP drops below SEK 24 (15% from ~SEK 28) FOLLOWED BY hyperscaler capex freeze or BEAD programme cancellation/major reallocation to satellite.

items.text: **5. NT/MT one-two invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any probable/actual near-term profit cut. HTRO-specific: SP drops below SEK 24 FOLLOWED BY Fibre Solutions margin falling

sub.text: below 5% or DC margin contracting below 12% (profitability squeeze from either segment).

blocks.text: **SP-conditional & composite invalidations (rules 6–10)** — sequence-dependent triggers (price action × fundamentals).

items.text: **6. Wisdom of crowd invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any plausible+ VF or actual SM threshold+ concerns re. __mid/long-term growth, margins, SRCAs__ or predictability thereof.

sub.text: HTRO-specific: SP <SEK 24 FOLLOWED BY evidence that DC services lack sustainable competitive advantage (e.g., Corning/Prysmian successfully enter mid-market DC services at scale, margin compression across DC peer group).

items.text: **7. Market catch-up with our existential concerns invalidation** — __VF plausible+ threshold+ concerns__ re. mid/long-term growth/margins/SRCAs FOLLOWED BY __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__. HTRO-specific:

sub.text: Watson identifies DC boom-bust pattern confirmation (e.g., hyperscaler order cancellations, DC services pricing deflation >10%) FOLLOWED BY SP <SEK 24.

items.text: **8. Narrow frame invalidation** — __Peerset underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any __actual cut/problem__. HTRO-specific: HTRO underperforms Nordic mid-cap industrials peer group by >15% FOLLOWED BY actual earnings

sub.text: cut, M&A write-down, or customer loss.

items.text: **9. SS EEG invalidation** — __[2]% or greater SS EPS cuts__ AND __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ (either order). HTRO-specific: consensus EPS cut of ≥2% (i.e., from SEK 2.25

sub.text: to SEK 2.20 or below) combined with SP <SEK 24.

items.text: **10. Case outputs/attributes invalidation** — __[8] or more case-output thresholds at D or F__. HTRO-specific: if 8+ of the IC#1 output CQs/TCs degrade to D or F ratings (e.g., EPS

sub.text: growth <5%, no upgrade path, multiple de-rates to <10x, case navigability collapses), the thesis is fundamentally broken regardless of SP.

blocks.text: _Escalation protocol: when a threshold trips, APM (i) records the trip in the case file with date and evidence, (ii) re-rates the affected pillar(s), (iii) escalates with __trigger evidence, thesis impact, recommended action__ (sell / trim X% / hold-with-tighter-stop) and reasoning chain, and (iv) flips the state in this section to TRIPPED. This sits __upstream of__ the 30-Day Shot Clock — the shot clock applies for deterioration without a tripped threshold; once a threshold trips, APM acts._

**Plausible invalidation scenarios (not yet ACH-level, but watchlist):**

(i) AI monetisation disappointment — if hyperscaler revenue growth from AI products disappoints (e.g., Meta/Google AI ad revenue plateaus), capex could be cut 20-30%, directly impacting DC fibre demand. Plausibility: 15-20% over N2Y.

(ii) FTTH permanent impairment — if North American FTTH demand never recovers to 2022 levels and BEAD is substantially reallocated to satellite/FWA, Fibre Solutions becomes a structurally declining business. Bear case terminal margin: 4-5% EBIT. Plausibility: 25-30% over N3Y.

(iii) M&A multiple inflation — PE competition for DC service companies inflates acquisition multiples to 10-12x EBITDA, making HTRO's 5-7x target unachievable and stalling the inorganic growth engine. Plausibility: 20% over N2Y.

**Short seller coverage:** No documented short-seller campaigns from major activist firms (Muddy Waters, Citron, Hindenburg, etc.). Specific short interest data (SI% of float, days to cover) not available in research corpus. Shareholder base dominated by institutional long-only investors (Handelsbanken Fonder 9.3%, Accendo Capital 5.9%).

**Bear case credibility: ORANGE.** The fundamental bearish pressures are real and documented: (i) FTTH boom-bust confirmed by profit warning July 2025, (ii) SEK 299m in non-recurring restructuring charges in H2-25, (iii) Redeye bear case fair value SEK 12-15 (40-50% downside). However: (i) no coordinated short campaign, (ii) DC segment accelerating not contracting, (iii) cash conversion exceptional. The bear case is a plausible downside scenario, not a fraud/accounting thesis.

items.text: **KOQ-1: DC switching costs — real or perceived?** Need ESA-depth evidence of customer lock-in: contract lengths, proprietary integration, service dependency, reference customer interviews. This is the moat question.
**KOQ-2: M&A pipeline quality.** What is the actual pipeline? How many targets identified? At what multiples? What is the integration failure rate historically? Weterings is a flag —

sub.text: is it an outlier or a pattern?

items.text: **KOQ-3: Fröberg's DC expertise.** Does the CEO understand the DC services market specifically, or is he applying an industrial wholesale playbook (Ahlsell) to a technology services market? What is his

sub.text: view on the boom-bust risk?

items.text: **KA-1: Progress to ESA.** Run full ESA-depth analysis on DC moat (KD#1) and M&A execution (KD#2). These are the gating questions.
**KA-2: Monitor Q1-26 DC print.** If DC organic >+30%, conviction increases; if <+15%, KC-1 moat concerns escalate. Print expected April 2026.
**KA-3: Technical check.** Run Minervini 8-point analysis. Confirm Stage 1→2 transition status. Set price alerts at SEK 24 (ACH trigger) and SEK 32 (breakout).

E.title: ACTIONS
E.inline_verdict: PROCEED to ESA — DC moat and M&A execution are gating questions

subsections.title: Pros/cons of prioritising vs other opportunities

blocks.text: **PROCEED to ESA.** Hexatronic passes the Triaging screen with a C+ overall IC rating. The case has genuine change forces (hyperscaler AI buildout, CEO-led restructuring, BEAD pipeline), measurable early outputs (DC +37% organic, 15%+ EBITA margin), and an attractive valuation entry point (12.5x forward P/E vs 15-18x for DC-services peers). The mix-shift narrative is credible and quantified.

**Why not higher than C+:** Two material uncertainties prevent a B- or above: (1) DC competitive moat is unproven — switching costs are asserted but not evidenced, and Corning/Prysmian could pivot to mid-market at scale. (2) M&A execution requires 12+ acquisitions over 3 years with no second Weterings-type capital destruction. Both of these are ESA-depth questions that cannot be resolved at Triaging.

**Sizing implication at Triaging:** Starter position only (1.0-1.5%) if Richard proceeds. Do NOT size beyond 1.5% before ESA delivers a defensible DC moat read. Stop-loss: -15% vs entry OR ACH-trip (whichever first).

items.text: Run ESA-depth analysis on KD#1 (DC moat) and KD#2 (M&A execution). These are the fulcrum questions.
Monitor Q1-26 DC organic print (expected April 2026). >+30% = conviction-building; <+15% = reassess.
Run Minervini 8-point technical analysis and confirm Stage 1→2 transition status.
If ESA passes: upsize to 2.0-2.5% on first confirming DC print + first M&A deal at claimed multiples. Cap at 3% until DD complete.
Set price alerts: SEK 24 (ACH trigger zone), SEK 32 (breakout above recent range).
If ESA fails (DC moat ORANGE, M&A execution RED): park to watchlist. Re-trigger if: (i) DC organic sustains >30% for 4 consecutive quarters, (ii) M&A track record improves (3+ deals

sub.text: with disclosed ROI), (iii) FTTH recovery materialises.

items.text: If DC boom-bust materialises: trim immediately to zero. Do not wait for recovery. FTTH cycle history shows 2+ year trough duration.
Alternative exposure: consider PRY (Prysmian) or NKT for cable/connectivity theme with better scale and vertical integration. Lower upside but lower risk.
